_meta.title: Snowflake Services Delivery Migration: J.D. Power Executive Presentation April 2026
_meta.description: Executive migration proposal for JD Power — BigQuery + Redshift → Snowflake. 8 workstreams, T&M + fixed-fee hybrid model, $2.08M partner fixed-fee. Reference deck for executive migration proposal pattern.

overview.heading: ENGAGEMENT OVERVIEW
overview.text: Total investment for an 18–24 month hybrid Professional Services engagement (T&M + fixed fee) spanning 8 workstreams. Tightly governed, with $2.08M contracted as Partner fixed-fee to eliminate overrun risk.

engagement.heading: MIGRATION SCOPE
engagement.subtitle: 8 workstreams covering full BigQuery + Redshift → Snowflake migration

workstreams.title: Discovery & Analysis
bullets: 181+ workspace inventory
Complexity scoring & migration briefs

workstreams.title: Platform & Governance
bullets: RBAC, CI/CD, FinOps, BCDR
Architecture advisory through delivery

workstreams.title: Data Ingestion
bullets: Replace all AIC connectors
Openflow CDC + Snowpipe patterns

workstreams.title: Historical Migration + Code Conversion
bullets: 1.25 PB BigQuery → Snowflake (DMVA)
120K+ SQL/PySpark tasks, Partner fixed-fee

outcomes.heading: THE OUTCOME
items: Guaranteed GCP spend eliminated ($136K/month) — 2.5-year payback period
Ends dual-platform overhead and establishes a single, highly governed platform
Full migration of active pipelines, decommissioning BigQuery and Redshift
Native Day-1 Snowflake Cortex AI capabilities delivered

cards.title: AIC V3 / BigQuery
bullets: 242 workspaces, 9,000+ pipelines
1.26 PB of data
~$136K/month in GCP spend
Primary platform driving cost and complexity
Board-mandated migration in 2026

cards.title: AIC V2 / Redshift
bullets: 120K+ SQL and PySpark tasks
600+ data connectors
Small number of active workloads
Critical upstream dependency for ~180 V3 connectors
Must migrate to enable full V3 cutover

cards.title: Content Parser + DTP Framework
bullets: Independent AI pipeline for document extraction
Not dependent on current platforms
Opportunity to deploy AI capabilities natively on Snowflake from Day 1
DTP: rebuilding dynamic pipeline creation capability in Snowflake

slides.title: Why Snowflake
slides.subtitle: Four strategic reasons this migration pays off beyond cost savings
slides.callout: Snowflake transforms JD Power's data platform by lowering cost, simplifying operations, and enabling AI-driven growth on a single, governed foundation.

cards.title: Reduce Operating Costs
bullets: Eliminate ~$1.6M/year in BigQuery spend
Remove dual-platform overhead and idle infrastructure waste
Shift to consumption-based model aligned to actual usage

cards.title: Enable AI-Driven Growth
bullets: Built-in AI capabilities available at go-live
No need for separate AI vendors or infrastructure
Accelerates time to deliver new data products and insights

cards.title: Accelerate Migration & Time to Value
bullets: Proven approach to migrate at scale with reduced manual effort
Faster delivery through automation and repeatable patterns
Minimizes business disruption during transition

cards.title: Strengthen Governance & Risk Control
bullets: Enterprise-grade security and data governance from Day 1
Improved cost visibility and accountability across teams
Simplified architecture reduces operational risk

phases.label:
Phase 0: Foundation
(Months 1–2)
phases.description: Establish the Snowflake platform and assess full migration scope
activities: Assess and prioritize all in-scope workloads
phases.outcome: Milestone: Platform ready and full migration scope confirmed

phases.label:
Phase 1: Pilot
(Months 1–2)
phases.description: Validate approach with a controlled, end-to-end migration — run in parallel with Foundation Phase
activities: Run in parallel with existing systems
Confirm performance, data accuracy, and business readiness
phases.outcome: Milestone: Pilot approved and migration approach validated

phases.label:
Phase 2: Scaled Delivery
(Month 3+)
phases.description: Execute full migration in parallel with minimal disruption
activities: Standardized approach ensures speed and consistency
Ongoing governance ensures quality and control
phases.outcome: Milestone: All workloads migrated and legacy platforms decommissioned

slides.title: What We're Migrating
slides.subtitle: 242 workspaces, 9,000+ pipelines | 1.26 PB of data | ~$136K/month in GCP spend | 120K+ SQL and PySpark tasks | 600+ data connectors

cards.title: AIC V3 / BigQuery
bullets: Primary platform driving cost and complexity
Board-mandated migration in 2026

cards.title: AIC V2 / Redshift
bullets: Must be migrated to enable full V3 cutover
Can run in parallel

cards.title: Content Parser
bullets: Opportunity to deploy AI capabilities natively on Snowflake from Day 1

cards.title: DTP Framework Migration
bullets: Rebuilding DTP dynamic pipeline creation capability in Snowflake

slides.title: Illustrative High Level Timeline
slides.subtitle: Parallel foundation and pilot phases enable a controlled scale-out to five teams, accelerating delivery with minimal disruption.

slides.title: Single Governance Model with Clear Accountability
slides.subtitle: Clear Ownership Model Reduces Execution Risk

cards.title: Snowflake SD
bullets: Role: Defines architecture and enforces quality gates across all phases
Accountable for: Platform design, standards, and quality
Why it matters: Ensures consistency, performance, and long-term scalability

cards.title: Partner (Datafold)
bullets: Role: Executes migration across dedicated delivery teams
Accountable for: Code conversion and delivery at scale
Why it matters: Fixed-fee model ensures predictable cost and delivery outcomes

cards.title: JD Power
bullets: Role: Owns UAT, parallel run, and go/no-go approval
Accountable for: Business validation and final cutover decision
Why it matters: Maintains full control over business continuity and risk

slides.title: Key Risks & How We Control Them
slides.callout: Risks are understood and structurally mitigated
left_bullets: Cost Overrun
Execution at Scale
Data Readiness
Business Disruption
right_bullets: Fixed-fee structure caps exposure
Pilot-first approach validates before scaling
Assessed and confirmed before execution
Parallel run and controlled cutover

slides.title: Engagement Summary
slides.subtitle: Initial Partner Estimates: Datafold $2.08M Fixed Fee | SoftServe $3.2M T&M for equivalent scope

slides.title: Critical Decision Required
slides.subtitle: Immediate decisions are required to unblock the May 2026 project kickoff.
bullets: Approve engagement scope and authorize Snowflake SD to proceed
Confirm partner selection: Datafold (fixed-fee) vs. SoftServe (T&M)
Designate JD Power program lead and cross-functional project team
Confirm CDC/logging readiness for on-prem database hosts (Data Ingestion go/no-go)
Confirm Streamlit UI approach: Greenfield ($186K) vs. Lift-and-Shift ($97K)

slides.title: RACI — Roles & Responsibilities by Workstream
slides.subtitle: R = Responsible  |  A = Accountable  |  C = Consulted  |  I = Informed

slides.title: Discovery & Analysis  |  $160K  |  Snowflake SD + Partner
left_bullets: 2-month duration, running in parallel with Platform Foundation and Migration Pilot
Inventory all 181+ workspaces: pipeline count, task types, connectors, stored GB
SQL/PySpark complexity scan — output IS the effort model for all downstream workstreams
Connector classification and Ingestion Pattern Design
Dependency mapping and downstream consumer catalog
Migration briefs authored and signed by workspace owners
Sequencing and prioritization plan for scaled delivery
Pilot included: First pilot workspace runs end-to-end during this phase
right_bullets: Duration: 2 months (parallel with Phase 0B Platform Foundation)
Scope: All 242 workspaces scanned; 181 migrating (estimate — may decrease)
Output: Complexity scores set hours for SQL & PySpark Conversions — cannot be skipped
Pilot runs: Full end-to-end including ingestion, migration, code conversion, UAT, deployment
Not everything will migrate: Discovery will rationalize the workspace list
Estimate assumes full scope; Phase 0A may reduce migrating workspace count
Partner (Datafold): $80K fixed fee  |  Snowflake SD: $80K
Total: $160K ($80K fixed fee)
slides.callout: Discovery is the effort model foundation. Cannot be skipped.

slides.title: Platform & Governance  |  $160K–$480K  |  Snowflake SD
left_bullets: RBAC framework: role hierarchy, service account roles per delivery team, per-workspace isolation
Privacy controls: Row Access Policies + Dynamic Data Masking tied to roles
FinOps cataloging and tagging (QUERY_TAG governance for cost attribution per team)
CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions → Dev → QA → Prod promotion framework
Business Continuity and Disaster Recovery (BCDR) architecture
Performance optimization and ongoing architecture advisory (extended engagement)
right_bullets: Base: 6 months — full foundation + security + BCDR architecture
Extended: 12 months ($320K) — adds performance optimization advisory
Extended: 18 months ($480K) — ongoing migration optimization and architecture advisory through scaled delivery
12–18 month option recommended if code conversion surfaces performance challenges
Extended option: Snowflake architect embedded throughout entire delivery
Snowflake SD only — no Partner component in any scenario
6-month base: $160K  |  12-month: $320K  |  18-month: $480K
slides.callout: Extending to 12–18 months is recommended if performance optimization is needed post-code-conversion.

slides.title: Data Ingestion  |  $125K–$200K  |  Snowflake SD
left_bullets: Replace all AIC connectors with Snowflake-native ingestion patterns
RDBMS: Openflow CDC for on-prem SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, Postgres sources
FTP/SFTP sources: External Stage + Snowpipe auto-ingest on file arrival
S3/GCS/Azure Blob: External Stage + Snowpipe with event notifications
External APIs (51 tasks): External Access Integration + Snowpark Stored Procedures
AICv2 connectors (180 repointed)
7-day post-go-live validation: row counts, freshness SLA, error alert verification
right_bullets: SCENARIO A — High ($200K): All on-prem databases have primary keys, CDC, and logging enabled. Openflow CDC pattern applies.
SCENARIO B — Low ($125K): JD Power pushes data to S3 or GCS. Snowflake SD ingests from cloud object storage.
Key ask: JD Power IT must confirm CDC/logging readiness for all on-prem hosts
Private IP hosts: PrivateLink or VPN required for SQL Server connectivity
High scenario: $200K  |  Low scenario: $125K  |  Owner: Snowflake SD only
slides.callout: On-prem database CDC readiness is the single biggest assumption in this workstream. JD Power IT confirmation required before finalizing cost.

slides.title: Historical Data Migration  |  $200K–$300K  |  Snowflake SD
left_bullets: Migrate 1.25 PB from BigQuery to Snowflake (primary volume)
Redshift (AIC V2) migration — significantly smaller footprint than BQ
DMVA (Data Migration & Validation Accelerator) — Snowflake internal Python utility
Automated unload + transfer + row-count validation in a single pass
Target DDL generation and type mapping (BQ → Snowflake, Redshift → Snowflake)
Delta catch-up loads after initial migration window
Migrated data available for beta access by end users during migration
right_bullets: Data volume: 1.25 PB BigQuery + materially smaller Redshift footprint
Tooling: DMVA automates transfer + validation at scale (internal Snowflake accelerator)
Beta use allowed: Migrated data suitable for testing and beta access during migration window
NOT for production: Do not run production SLAs against DMVA-loaded data until pipeline cutover completes
Covers both BQ (V3 workspaces) and Redshift (V2 workspaces) migration tracks
High: $300K  |  Low: $200K  |  Owner: Snowflake SD only
slides.callout: DMVA is an internal Snowflake accelerator — not a 3rd-party product. Data loaded via DMVA is suitable for beta review but should not power production SLAs during migration.

slides.title: Code Conversion + Testing + Orchestration  |  $2.4M  |  Partner (SD Oversight)
left_bullets: Full conversion of all SQL, BQ_Script, PySpark, R, JS, Kotlin pipeline tasks
Unit testing for every converted script (row count + sample row validation)
Orchestration: AIC pipeline JSON → Snowflake Task DAGs (~70% auto via CoCo templates)
End-to-end ETL testing including parallel run issue resolution
30-day parallel run coordination and divergence root cause resolution
Full conversion guarantee: code accepted by business before any cutover
Demos, knowledge transfer, and JD Power team enablement throughout
right_bullets: Partner executes at scale; Snowflake SD serves as SME and pattern authority
~95% of V3 PySpark runs on Snowpark Connect with minimal changes (GA Nov 2025)
~5% requiring RDD/Spark ML rewrites handled by Snowflake SD specialist team
R tasks (645 instances): Python rewrite or SPCS container decision per script
SnowConvert for SQL; CoCo templates for PySpark PS1/PS2 pattern application
Partner (fixed fee): $2,000K  |  Snowflake SD: $400K  |  Total: $2,400K
Partner line item is fixed fee — no overruns, full outcome guarantee
slides.callout: Fixed-fee, outcome-based delivery. Partner guarantees conversion quality. Snowflake SD guarantees architecture and patterns are correct.

slides.title: UAT · Deployment · Repointing · Change Management  |  $0  |  JD Power Owned
left_bullets: UAT: Business owners validate 5–10 key outputs per workspace in Snowflake vs BigQuery
Sign-off gate: 30-day zero sev-1 parallel run required before any cutover
Deployment: JD Power executes promotion from staging to production
Downstream repointing: BI tools, Tableau, APIs, cross-workspace AIC references
Change management: Communications to end users, downstream consumers, stakeholders
JD Power owns the cutover decision and 30-day rollback window management
right_bullets: Snowflake SD: Informed on UAT progress; consulted if technical issues arise
Snowflake SD: Ensures all code is production-ready before staging handoff
Snowflake SD: Provides rollback runbook; participates in go/no-go review
Partner: Supports UAT environment; investigates root cause on divergences
Recommended: JD Power designates a dedicated deployment team per cohort
Optional add-on: Datafold automated repointing available (~$100K–$250K if needed)
slides.callout: Engagement cost for these four workstreams: $0. JD Power owns execution. Snowflake SD is consulted as needed and informed throughout.

slides.title: DTP Framework Migration  |  $220K–$340K  |  Snowflake SD
left_bullets: Full rebuild of DTP (Data Processing Platform) capabilities natively on Snowflake
Not a lift-and-shift — pipeline automation and orchestration intelligence moves fully onto platform
Primary deliverable: Snowflake Task DAGs for pipeline orchestration
2,175 DTP jobs in scope across the 2 T3-DTP workspaces
Snowflake SD data engineering architect skill set required
DPT specialist team runs independently — does not compete with Teams 1–5
right_bullets: This is a platform rebuild — NOT a standard workspace migration
Current DPT has pipeline automation + creation + intelligence running outside Snowflake
Goal: entire workload happens natively on Snowflake with no external orchestration dependencies
Main effort: Tasks and DAGs for pipeline orchestration at scale
Sizing depends on DAG complexity confirmed during Phase 0A Discovery
Low: $220K  |  Estimated $300K  |  High: $340K  |  Owner: Snowflake SD data engineering architects only
slides.callout: DPT is treated as a platform rebuild, not a migration. Most architecturally complex component — runs on a dedicated Snowflake SD specialist track.

slides.title: Content Parser AI + Streamlit UI  |  $157K–$343K  |  Snowflake SD
left_bullets: Content Parser AI ($157K): Cortex AI COMPLETE() extraction pipeline for Incentive + Core Car Data document types
Prompt management framework: registry tables, version history, sample-based testing harness
Accuracy testing against JD Power historical ground truth records
Multi-schema support: North America + Europe regional variants
Streamlit UI — Option A Greenfield ($186K): Full 12-screen UI including template builder, jobs management, prompt editor, RBAC
Streamlit UI — Option B Lift-and-Shift ($97K): Replatform existing UI to Snowflake-native Streamlit
right_bullets: JD Power provides full prompt library (base + OEM supplemental) at kickoff
JD Power provides representative document samples for both doc types before build begins
Scoped to 2 document types only: Incentive + Core Car Data
HITL review UI, action-taking agents, drag-and-drop workflow UI are out of scope
UI approach (Greenfield vs Lift-and-Shift) must be confirmed before SOW
Content Parser AI: $157K (constant in both scenarios)
Greenfield: $157K + $186K = $343K  |  Lift-and-Shift: $157K + $97K = $254K
slides.callout: Greenfield vs Lift-and-Shift is an $89K decision. Recommend confirming approach before final SOW. AI pipeline cost ($157K) is the same in both scenarios.

slides.title: Pricing Assumptions
slides.subtitle: Four immediate decisions are required to unblock the May 2026 project kickoff.
bullets: All pricing is estimated and subject to final scoping and SOW execution
Partner (Datafold) pricing shown: fixed-fee model eliminates overrun risk
Snowflake SD pricing is T&M unless noted as fixed-fee
Platform & Governance pricing varies by engagement duration: 6/12/18 months
Data Ingestion scenario (High vs Low) depends on JD Power IT CDC/logging confirmation
Historical Migration range depends on Redshift footprint confirmation
DTP sizing depends on DAG complexity confirmed in Phase 0A
Content Parser UI approach (Greenfield vs Lift-and-Shift) pending JD Power decision

slides.title: Thank You
slides.subtitle: Snowflake Services Delivery
slides.message: We look forward to partnering with JD Power on this migration journey.
